critical_issues: D10 12-month projection cumulative spend ($23,580) is irreconcilable with the break-even analysis total annual operating cost ($29,260–$35,260 for Year 1 at 250 clients). The ~$6,000–$12,000 gap matches the developer retainer, suggesting the 12-month projection silently excludes it while the break-even analysis includes it. The 'Monthly Cost' column header gives no indication of what is included or excluded, creating a misleading budget planning artifact.
contradictions: D55-P8-015 establishes updated service fee pricing ($150 solo, $125 pairs, $100 for groups of 4+) that contradicts D8 FC-058 seed data defaults ($125 for 2-3, $100 for 4-6, $75 for 7+). A Phase 7 developer building FC-058 will use the D8 defaults; the Phase 8 pricing decision has no mechanism to reach FC-058's acceptance criteria.

Methodology feedback item #3 states 'Phase 5 estimated Stripe at ~4.5–5% effective rate' but D5 Part 3 explicitly states '~5.5–6.0%' with a full component breakdown. The ~4.5–5% figure originates from Phase 1 (D1 Section 6.3), not Phase 5. This misattribution produces an incorrect root-cause analysis.

D9 Resend API Key is placed in the 'Shared Operations' vault (shared with Lucia) but the credential management section classifies it as a secret that 'must NEVER be committed' and its environment variable (RESEND_API_KEY) is server-side only. All other server-side secrets (Supabase Service Role Key, Stripe Secret Key, Stripe Webhook Secret, Sentry Auth Token) are in Rafael-only vaults. The Resend key should be in 'Platform Infrastructure' for consistency with the stated security model.

D9 UptimeRobot setup (Section 10) instructs creating 2 monitors, but D18 Section 2 specifies 3 monitors (adding Supabase Status at status.supabase.com with 5-minute interval). The D9 setup guide will produce an incomplete monitoring configuration relative to D18's specification.
warnings: D15 SEO spec enumerates page titles for only 11 routes, while D7's page inventory defines 34 MVP pages. Missing routes include /bookings/new, /clients/new, /clients/leads/[id], /clients/leads/new, /courses/new, /network/[id], /network/new, and /bookings/[id]/proposals/new. Browser tab titles for these pages are unspecified.

D15 references First Input Delay (FID) as a performance target, but FID was deprecated as a Core Web Vital in favor of Interaction to Next Paint (INP) in March 2024. For a document dated April 2026, this is outdated guidance.

D10 12-month projection 'Monthly Cost' column values do not match any transparent formula of infrastructure + Stripe processing. For example, November 2026 shows $1,510 but infrastructure ($68.50) + Stripe (15 × $1,600 × 5.6% = $1,344) = $1,412.50. The calculation basis is not explained, making the projection difficult to validate or update.

D10 cross-reference manifest uses generic field names (cost_1k_users, cost_50k_users) that do not match the actual scale tiers in the document (Year 1 ~250 clients, Year 3 ~650 clients). These appear to be unmodified template defaults.

D17 Email #1 specifies merge variable {{client.first_name}} but D4's clients table defines only full_name (no first_name field). The derivation logic (splitting full_name) is not specified, which could lead to incorrect rendering for clients with multi-part first names or non-Western name structures.

D20 Section 3 item 4 references 'D5 Part 4' for security advisory checks, but the task described (npm audit) relates more directly to D5 Part 5 (Deployment & DevOps). The reference is not wrong but could misdirect a developer looking for the relevant context.
